# Basic MCP Filesystem Server Test
# Tests server startup, initialization, and basic protocol handling

# Build the filesystem server binary
go build -o mcp-filesystem-server .

# Create a temporary test directory
mkdir test_dir
cd test_dir

# Test basic MCP protocol initialization with current directory as allowed
exec ../mcp-filesystem-server . <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{"roots":{"listChanged":true},"sampling":{}}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/list","params":{},"id":2}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"ping","params":{},"id":3}
EOF

# Verify server information in initialization response
stdout '"name":"filesystem-server"'
stdout '"version":"1.0.0"'

# Verify capabilities are reported
stdout '"tools"'

# Verify all filesystem tools are listed
stdout '"name":"read_file"'
stdout '"description":"Read the contents of a file"'
stdout '"name":"write_file"'
stdout '"description":"Write content to a file"'
stdout '"name":"list_directory"'
stdout '"description":"List the contents of a directory"'
stdout '"name":"get_file_info"'
stdout '"description":"Get information about a file or directory"'

# Verify ping response
stdout '"result":"pong"'

# Cleanup
cd ..
rm -rf test_dir
